chapter_title.en: Chapter 63 – The Divorce of disavowment by payment (Al-Mubarat)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/240)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/240)
text: الْمُبَارِئَةُ تَقُولُ لِزَوْجِهَا لَكَ مَا عَلَيْكَ وَ بَارِئْنِي وَ يَتْرُكُهَا قَالَ قُلْتُ فَيَقُولُ لَهَا فَإِنِ ارْتَجَعْتِ فِي شَيْ‏ءٍ فَأَنَا أَمْلَكُ بِبُضْعِكِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from Muhammad Bin Ziyad, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said, ‘Al-Mubaraat – She is saying to her husband, ‘For you is what (debt) is upon you, and disavow me’, and he leaves her’. I said, ‘So he is saying to her, ‘So if you retract with regards to something, so I am in control with coupling with you?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 6. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from ibn Sama‘ah from Muhammad ibn Ziyad from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that in al-Mubarat (disavowing) a woman says to her husband, ’You can have whatever you owe to me and free me (from the bonds of marriage). He then sets her free (from the bonds of marriage).’ I (the narrator) then asked, ’Will he then say to her, "If you demand anything back then I will have more right than anyone else to have you as my wife”?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can say so.’”
